name: iChat.today
tagline: An autonomous AI agent for your browser
description: Give it a goal and it works the web for you. Not a chat sidebar — a full browser copilot that navigates, clicks, fills forms, and gets things done across any website.
problem: You spend hours doing repetitive browser tasks manually

name: iScan.today
tagline: Catch leaked secrets before hackers do
description: Scans GitHub, GitLab, NPM, Docker Hub and more for accidentally exposed API keys, tokens, and credentials. Real-time alerts so you can revoke before damage is done.
problem: Leaked API keys can cost you thousands before you notice

name: iBlockBots.today
tagline: Auto-detect and block AI reply-farming bots on X
description: Tired of AI-generated spam replies under your tweets? This Chrome extension detects and blocks bot accounts automatically so your timeline stays human.
problem: AI bots are ruining Twitter/X replies with spam

name: iLink.today
description: Share passwords, keys, or sensitive text through links that auto-expire after being viewed. End-to-end encrypted — even the server can't read your content.
problem: Sharing passwords over Slack or email is a security risk

name: iSsl.today
description: Monitor your SSL certificates and get Slack/Discord alerts before they expire. Also detects dangling DNS records vulnerable to subdomain takeover.
problem: Expired SSL certs break trust and SEO overnight

name: GhRev.com
tagline: Give your AI CLI a seat in every GitHub PR
description: Chrome extension that integrates AI-powered code review directly into GitHub pull requests. Get instant feedback, suggestions, and security checks without leaving your workflow.
problem: Switching between CLI and GitHub for code review is tedious

name: iPing.today
tagline: Instant cha-ching notifications for LemonSqueezy sales
description: Get real-time push notifications with a satisfying cha-ching sound every time you make a sale. Because indie makers deserve to celebrate every purchase.
problem: You miss the dopamine hit when a sale happens while you're away

name: iWatcher.watch
tagline: Analyze and verify GitHub access tokens
description: Paste a GitHub token and instantly see what permissions it has, what repos it can access, and whether it's been compromised. Essential for security audits.
problem: You found a GitHub token but have no idea what it can access

name: Pocketbase
tagline: Open source backend in a single file
description: Realtime database, auth, file storage, and admin dashboard — all in one executable. No dependencies, no Docker, just download and run. SQLite under the hood.
problem: Setting up a backend takes days of boilerplate

name: Plausible Analytics
description: Lightweight, open source web analytics. No cookies, no personal data collection, fully GDPR compliant. Script is under 1KB — won't slow your site down.
problem: Google Analytics is bloated, invasive, and overkill for most sites

name: Typesense
tagline: Open source search engine that just works
description: Typo-tolerant, blazing fast search engine you can self-host. Drop-in alternative to Algolia. Set up in 30 seconds, not 30 hours.
problem: Algolia is expensive and you don't need enterprise search

name: Dub.co
tagline: Open source link management for modern marketers
description: Short links with built-in analytics, custom domains, and team collaboration. API-first so you can integrate it anywhere. Used by Vercel, Hashnode, and others.
problem: Bitly charges a fortune and you can't self-host it

name: Tolgee
tagline: Localize your app without bugging developers
description: Open source i18n platform. Translators can edit strings directly in your running app with in-context editing. Supports React, Vue, Angular, and more.
problem: Managing translation JSON files across languages is a nightmare

name: Documenso
description: E-signing that's transparent and self-hostable. Send documents for signature, track status, and keep full control of your data. No vendor lock-in.
problem: DocuSign costs $25/mo just to send a few documents

name: Novu
description: Unified API for push, email, SMS, in-app, and chat notifications. Build once, deliver everywhere. Content management and subscriber preferences built in.
problem: Building notifications for 5 channels means 5 different integrations

name: Trigger.dev
description: Write long-running tasks in TypeScript, deploy to their cloud. Retries, scheduling, and observability included. Like a modern, developer-friendly alternative to Sidekiq or Bull.
problem: Managing Redis + Bull + workers just to run background jobs

name: Cal.com
description: Scheduling infrastructure for everyone. Self-hostable, API-first, and packed with integrations. Round-robin, collective scheduling, and custom workflows.
problem: Calendly charges $12/mo for basic scheduling features

name: Infisical
description: Sync environment variables and secrets across your team and CI/CD. End-to-end encrypted, with audit logs and access controls. Replace .env files for good.
problem: Sharing .env files over Slack is how secrets get leaked

name: Hoppscotch
description: Lightweight alternative to Postman. Test REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, and SSE APIs right in your browser. Fast, beautiful, and no account required to start.
problem: Postman became bloated and requires an account for everything

name: Appwrite
tagline: Open source backend server for web & mobile
description: Auth, databases, storage, functions, and messaging in one platform. Self-host or use cloud. SDKs for every language. Build your backend in minutes, not months.
problem: Firebase locks you in and pricing is unpredictable

name: Resend
description: Send transactional and marketing emails with a modern API. React Email for templates, webhooks for tracking. Built by the creator of react-email.
problem: SendGrid and Mailgun APIs feel like they were designed in 2010

name: Supabase
description: Postgres database, auth, edge functions, realtime subscriptions, storage, and vector embeddings. Everything you need to build, all from one dashboard.
problem: Firebase locks you into Google's ecosystem with no exit plan

name: Mintlify
description: Write docs in MDX, get a polished documentation site with search, analytics, and API playground. Used by Stripe-quality docs without Stripe-sized teams.
problem: Writing great docs is hard and ugly docs drive users away

name: Lago
tagline: Open source metering and usage-based billing
description: Track usage events, apply pricing models, and generate invoices. Self-hostable Stripe Billing alternative for usage-based and hybrid pricing.
problem: Stripe Billing can't handle usage-based pricing well

name: Eraser
tagline: Docs and diagrams for engineering teams
description: Whiteboard, document editor, and diagram-as-code in one tool. AI generates architecture diagrams from your codebase. Replaces Notion + Miro + Lucidchart.
problem: Your architecture diagrams are scattered across 3 different tools

name: Polar
description: Sell subscriptions, license keys, digital downloads, and accept donations. Built specifically for open source maintainers and indie devs. Integrates with GitHub.
problem: Monetizing open source projects is awkward and fragmented

name: Unkey
description: Create, validate, and manage API keys at the edge. Rate limiting, usage tracking, and key expiry built in. Sub-millisecond verification globally.
problem: Rolling your own API key auth is error-prone and slow

name: Papermark
description: Share pitch decks and documents with real-time analytics. See who viewed, how long per page, and get notified on opens. Self-hostable.
problem: DocSend charges $15/mo to share a PDF with view tracking

name: Formbricks
tagline: Open source survey platform for product teams
description: In-app surveys, website popups, and link surveys with targeting and segmentation. Privacy-first, GDPR compliant, self-hostable.
problem: Typeform is expensive and sends your user data to their servers

name: Twenty
description: A modern alternative to Salesforce. Clean UI, GraphQL API, self-hostable. Manage contacts, companies, deals, and tasks without the enterprise bloat.
problem: Salesforce costs $300/seat and takes 6 months to set up
